encounter.reason: Nasal congestion and cough for 3 days; annual influenza vaccination
encounter.location: Cascade Community Health Center, 8205 SE Foster Road, Portland, OR 97206

conditions.name: acute viral upper respiratory infection
conditions.note: 3-day history of nasal congestion, mild cough, and low-grade fever. Exam consistent with viral URI — erythematous nasal mucosa, mild pharyngeal erythema, lungs clear. No bacterial superinfection.

conditions.name: mild intermittent asthma
conditions.note: Currently stable despite URI. Increased albuterol use every other day this week, up from baseline 1-2 times/month. No wheezing on exam, lungs clear. No escalation of therapy required.

conditions.name: overweight
conditions.note: BMI 27.4 consistent with prior range. Not specifically addressed at this encounter beyond vital sign documentation.

medications.name: albuterol sulfate 90 mcg/actuation HFA inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs by oral inhalation as needed for shortness of breath or wheezing; may use up to every 4-6 hours as needed during URI

immunizations.name: influenza vaccine, inactivated, quadrivalent, 2025-2026 formulation
immunizations.note: Administered despite mild URI per CDC guidance. Patient tolerated well. Vaccine Information Statement provided. Documented in immunization registry.

document_references.description: Office visit note documenting evaluation of 3-day acute viral upper respiratory infection (nasal congestion, cough, low-grade fever), physical exam findings (erythematous nasal mucosa, mild pharyngeal erythema, clear lungs), assessment of stable asthma despite URI with increased albuterol use, symptomatic management plan, and administration of influenza vaccine (quadrivalent, 2025-2026 formulation) with documentation that vaccination is appropriate despite mild URI.
